description: Main project overview and command guide for the pre-Zotero BatLit deduplication pipeline.
Prevents local PDFs extracted text generated outputs and other large or private workflow artifacts from being committed accidentally.
GitHub project metadata and issue templates or workflows if added.
Local project planning notes and GitHub issue drafts or records.
Human-readable descriptions of folders and files in this project. This is not a PDF-level manifest.
Main working directory for the BatLit pre-Zotero deduplication pipeline.
Drop zone for a newly supplied collection of PDFs before screening. Current collection is Bates 2026 shared by Paul Bates in 2026.
Lightweight comparison indexes used before Zotero import including BatLit reference exports such as refs.csv.
Lightweight BatLit fingerprint index used for pre-Zotero comparison. Includes DOI title authors year normalized fields Zotero links and available attachment MD5 hashes.
Latest and timestamped CSV/RIS reports produced by dedupe routing DOI extraction metadata staging and related checks.
Cached extracted text and intermediate files used by scripts. These are reproducible working files not final review outputs.
Timestamped manifests and diffs for incoming batches used to record what changed when a new collection was added.
Before/after Zotero collection comparison outputs used after deduplicated items are added to Zotero.
Optional downstream citation-network outputs including cited-reference candidate spreadsheets and edge lists extracted from routed PDFs.
Older active/mixed processed folders. Prefer processed_runs/ for clean timestamped review outputs.
Clean timestamped routing outputs. Each run keeps its own duplicates likely duplicates new literature manual review and non-bat review folders.
Collection-level action logs and summaries that record what happened to each file in a named collection.
Reusable Python scripts that run the pipeline.
PDFs from Bates 2026 that matched the existing BatLit corpus or another item in the incoming batch by exact file hash or DOI. Do not import as new Zotero literature.
PDFs from Bates 2026 that are high-confidence possible duplicates generally from title author and year matching. Human confirmation is needed before import.
PDFs from Bates 2026 that did not match BatLit or another incoming-batch item and passed the current bat-relevance screen. These are candidates for Zotero ingestion after metadata review.
Duplicate-omitted copy of Bates 2026 new_literature for next-stage metadata review and Zotero ingestion.
PDFs from Bates 2026 sorted out because extracted text did not show bat-relevance terms such as bat bats or chiroptera and/or showed non-bat context terms. Review for scope before import.
Duplicate-omitted Bates 2026 likely duplicate review set. These are possible duplicates that need manual confirmation before any Zotero import.
Ambiguous PDFs such as files with extraction failures or insufficient metadata that need a manual decision before import. May be absent or empty in a run.
Files that could not be processed cleanly enough for routine routing and need better OCR repair or manual citation searching. May be absent or empty in a run.
Category-specific bibliography and routing metadata for PDFs in that category folder.
Excel version of the category-specific bibliography and routing metadata.
Report showing which routed PDFs had metadata embedded into PDF document info fields.
One row per Bates 2026 incoming PDF recording action taken routed folder duplicate reference decision reason metadata hashes and BatLit match fields.
Compact summary of Bates 2026 decisions and routing actions.
Creates timestamped manifests and diffs when a new set of PDFs is added to incoming/.
Builds the lightweight BatLit literature fingerprint index from refs.csv for pre-Zotero comparison.
Screens incoming PDFs against BatLit and the current incoming batch using hashes DOI extraction title author year matching and bat-relevance terms.
Copies or moves screened PDFs into review folders and creates per-folder bibliography CSV/XLSX files.
Creates duplicate-omitted new literature and likely duplicate review folders plus manifest CSVs for a processed run.
Creates the collection-level action log and summary for a named collection such as Bates 2026.
Embeds routed bibliography metadata into PDF document info fields for Zotero-friendly import.
Reports PDFs with missing or suspicious title author year or text extraction results.
Extracts cited-reference candidates from routed PDFs and writes a track-down spreadsheet plus citation edge list.
Produces DOI-focused diagnostics for extracted DOI candidates.
Creates a Zotero RIS staging file for candidate new literature.
Compares before/after Zotero collection exports and writes added removed changed and unchanged reports.
